facts_disclosed: Traded BMW X5 for Lexus RX

turns.content: Can you walk me through how to set up Apple CarPlay in a Lexus? I just got a new car and the technology is different from what I had before.

Of course. For most recent Lexus models, the process is fairly straightforward:

1. **Start the car** and make sure the infotainment screen is on.
2. **Connect your iPhone** to the car's USB port using a Lightning or USB-C cable (depending on your iPhone model). It needs to be a data-capable cable, not just a charging cable.
3. On the Lexus touchscreen, look for a **"Phone"** or **"Apple CarPlay"** option — it may appear automatically when the phone is plugged in.
4. On your iPhone, you may see a prompt asking if you want to allow CarPlay. Tap **"Allow."**
5. Once connected, the CarPlay interface should appear on the Lexus screen with your familiar apps — Maps, Messages, Phone, Music, etc.

Which Lexus model did you get? The setup can vary slightly depending on whether it has the newer touchscreen interface or the older trackpad system.

It is the RX. I traded in my BMW — ay, I was tired of dealing with that car. This one is much simpler so far, except I cannot figure out the infotainment system. The BMW had iDrive which was its own nightmare, but at least I had finally learned it.

The Lexus RX is a good choice — and yes, switching between infotainment systems is always a learning curve. The good news is that once you get CarPlay running, you'll mostly interact with the Apple interface rather than the Lexus one, which simplifies everything.

If you have a 2023 or newer RX, it has the newer 14-inch touchscreen and the setup is very intuitive — plug in, tap allow, done. The older RX models (2022 and earlier) used a trackpad controller that was notoriously frustrating, but the new ones moved to a full touchscreen.

Do you know what year your RX is? And when you plug your phone in, does anything appear on the screen at all, or is it completely unresponsive?
It is a 2025. And when I plug it in it charges the phone but nothing comes up on the screen. I might be using the wrong port — there are two USB ports in the center console.

That's almost certainly the issue. In the 2025 RX, one USB port is for data (CarPlay) and the other is charge-only. They look identical, which is a poor design choice.

Try this:

1. Unplug from the current port.
2. Plug into the **other** USB port in the console.
3. Wait a few seconds — you should see a CarPlay prompt appear on the touchscreen.

The data port is typically the one closer to the front of the console, but it varies. If neither wired port works, also check that CarPlay is enabled on your iPhone: go to **Settings > General > CarPlay** and make sure the Lexus appears as an available vehicle.

Also confirm Siri is turned on — CarPlay requires Siri to be enabled even if you never use it.

Let me check. Hold on... I switched ports and now there is a prompt on the screen. It says 'Enable Apple CarPlay?' I tapped yes and now it is showing my apps. That was the other port. Why they do not label these things is beyond me.

Glad that worked. And you're completely right — there's no good reason not to label them. It's one of those things that's obvious to the engineers and baffling to everyone else.

Now that it's connected, a few tips for using CarPlay in the RX:

- **Maps** — You can use Apple Maps, Google Maps, or Waze through CarPlay. Just open whichever you prefer on the touchscreen.
- **Messages** — Siri will read incoming texts aloud and you can dictate replies without touching the phone.
- **Music/Podcasts** — Whatever you play on your phone will come through the car speakers.
- **The physical volume knob** on the center console still controls volume, even in CarPlay mode. You don't have to use the touchscreen for that.

How are you finding the RX otherwise, compared to the BMW?

Night and day. The BMW was beautiful, I will give it that, but that car was in the shop constantly. The electronics, the sensors, something was always malfunctioning. Carlos told me for years to switch to something more reliable and I resisted because I liked how the BMW looked. Finally I listened to him. Claro, as much as it pains me to admit, he was right.

That's a very common BMW-to-Lexus story. BMWs are engineered to impress, Lexuses are engineered to just work. The RX specifically has one of the best reliability records in its class — you should see a dramatic reduction in those shop visits.

And the 2025 RX is no slouch in the looks department either. Lexus has really updated the exterior design in the last couple of years. You're not sacrificing style for reliability the way you might have five or ten years ago.

Carlos sounds like a practical man. That kind of no-nonsense thinking saves a lot of headaches.

He is the most practical person I have ever known. No drama, no fuss, just 'does it work or does it not work.' He drove a Toyota Tacoma for fourteen years and only traded it in because the frame was rusting. That is his standard for when to replace a vehicle — structural failure.

Fourteen years and a rusting frame — that man sets a high bar for vehicle retirement. Though keeping a Toyota running that long doesn't take much effort, which is sort of the point. The Lexus is Toyota's luxury division, so you're essentially getting Carlos's reliability philosophy wrapped in a nicer interior.

He's probably feeling very vindicated right now.

Oh, he is insufferable about it. Every time I say something positive about the Lexus he gets this look. He does not even have to say 'I told you so' — the look is enough. Forty years of marriage, he has perfected that look.

Forty years earns you the right to the silent 'I told you so.' That's a marital privilege. At least he's gracious enough not to say it out loud — that shows restraint.

Is there anything else about the RX's tech you want to sort out while we're at it? The navigation, the safety features, the driver profiles? Better to get it all figured out now rather than discovering things one frustration at a time.

Not today. You have solved the main problem — I needed the CarPlay working because I use the GPS every day to get to the courthouse. Miami traffic, you know. You need real-time routing or you will lose an hour of your life on the Palmetto. Thank you for your patience with me on this.

You're very welcome. With CarPlay running, Waze or Google Maps will reroute you in real time around whatever the Palmetto is doing on any given day. That alone makes the setup worth it. Enjoy the new RX — and if Carlos gives you the look again, just remind him it took his recommendation to get you there. Shared credit.
